error.msg: Struct 'filesystem_error' has a constructor with 1 argument that is not explicit.
error.verbose: Struct 'filesystem_error' has a constructor with 1 argument that is not explicit. Such, so called "Converting constructors", should in general be explicit for type safety reasons as that prevents unintended implicit conversions.

error.msg: %s in format string (no. 1) requires 'char *' but the argument type is 'std::string'.
error.verbose: %s in format string (no. 1) requires 'char *' but the argument type is 'std::string'.

C-style pointer casting detected. C++ offers four different kinds of casts as replacements: static_cast, const_cast, dynamic_cast and reinterpret_cast. A C-style cast could evaluate to any of those automatically, thus it is considered safer if the programmer explicitly states which kind of cast is expected.

C-style pointer casting detected. C++ offers four different kinds of casts as replacements: static_cast, const_cast, dynamic_cast and reinterpret_cast. A C-style cast could evaluate to any of those automatically, thus it is considered safer if the programmer explicitly states which kind of cast is expected.

error.msg: Function parameter 'new_value' should be passed by const reference.
error.verbose: Parameter 'new_value' is passed by value. It could be passed as a const reference which is usually faster and recommended in C++.

location.info: Function parameter 'new_value' should be passed by const reference.

error.msg: Parameter 'entry' can be declared as reference to const
error.verbose: Parameter 'entry' can be declared as reference to const

location.info: Parameter 'entry' can be declared as reference to const

error.msg: Passing the result of c_str() to a function that takes std::string as argument no. 2 is slow and redundant.
error.verbose: The conversion from const char* as returned by c_str() to std::string creates an unnecessary string copy or length calculation. Solve that by directly passing the string.
